title: Two arrested for sexual assault-cum-murder of minor girl in Sulur near Coimbatore
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay expressed shock over the incident and said he has instructed the police to conduct a swift and thorough investigation

title: Police intensify checking fitness of school, college buses in Vijayawada
content: DCP instructs drivers to keep the buses fit, maintain first-aid kits and fire-fighting equipment in the vehicles

title: Retail fuel breaches ₹115-mark in Andhra Pradesh as successive hikes pinch consumers
content: The rapid sequence of retail corrections, which marks a cumulative hike of over ₹5.50 per litre in a little over a week, has severely disrupted operational calculations for independent dealers and logistics providers

title: ‘Bay of Bapatla’ coffee mugs, caps to promote beach tourism in A.P.
content: The initiative aims to strengthen identity of the coastal destination and enhance tourist engagement through locally inspired merchandise

title: Organisations and environmentalists demand withdrawal of CJI’s remarks on activists
content: The letter said that a responsible democracy does not ask citizens to choose between development and environment

title: Marco Rubio in India LIVE: Rubio invites PM Modi to White House
content: The two leaders discussed sustained progress in the India-U.S. Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership and issues related to regional and global peace and security

title: Name on SSLC certificate valid unless changed via gazette, says Kerala State Human Rights Commission
content: Observation made while considering a petition filed by a former woman employee of Pala Marketing Cooperative Society

title: Major changes announced in policing Kerala; Operation Toofan to be launched on June 1
content: 'Operation Toofan – The Narco Hunt’ is a major anti-drug drive by the Kerala Police aimed at eradicating drug networks and curbing the exploitation of students by narcotics rackets

title: AIIMS Delhi forms 4-member board for Twisha Sharma's second autopsy, team to fly to Bhopal
content: Chief of Forensic Medicine at AIIMS Delhi, Dr. Sudhir Gupta, said the court order authorised the director of AIIMS Delhi to constitute the medical board

title: Pemmasani asks youth to focus on skills at Rozgar Mela
content: Union Minister says transparent recruitment and infrastructure development are accelerating India’s progress towards becoming a developed nation

title: Rupamoni Gorh becomes first woman from Assam to scale Mount Everest
content: "Assam’s daughter has reached the top of the world. Heartiest congratulations to Rupamoni Gorh for becoming the first woman from Assam to scale Mount Everest as part of the ITBP all-women expedition team," he said in a social media post

title: HC seeks report on steps to rehabilitate people who were acquitted of crimes due to unsoundness of mind
content: Such persons cannot be allowed to languish indefinitely in mental health institutions after recovery, observes court

title: Study maps Assam’s grim human-elephant conflict
content: Analysing 1,806 incidents between 2000 and 2023, the researchers find that the conflicts near fragmented forests and human settlements occur more during the monsoon and post-harvest seasons

title: University of Kerala inks agreement for international project to study cooperative behavior between dolphins and humans
content: Researchers will use hydrophones, drones, and photo-documentation to study dolphin interactions with each other and fishers. This investigation aims to explore how these human-wildlife interactions inform the evolution of cooperative behaviors, which are rare across species.

title: Goyal to visit Canada next week to boost FTA talks, investments
content: The Minister will call on Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and hold a bilateral meeting with his Canadian counterpart Maninder Sidhu

title: ‘Sanjeevani’ will be implemented across State by August 15, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: The healthcare initiative will facilitate doorstep delivery of 74 types of medical tests, says the Chief Minster, adding that citizens will soon have access to an ‘AI Doctor’ for medical consultation from home

title: West Bengal gets ₹3,000 crore for healthcare revamp, Ayushman Bharat card rollout from July: CM Suvendu Adhikari
content: The CM said the State is planning to ink an agreement with the Centre in Delhi for inclusion in the Ayushman Arogya Mandir network to extend the benefits of Ayushman Bharat to its migrant residents

title: Ebola surveillance system launched at Bhubaneswar airport
content: An Ebola surveillance system has been launched at Biju Patnaik International Airport to strengthen health screening and monitoring measures for international passengers

title: BRS desperate to damage Hyderabad’s image: Sridhar Babu
content: Politics is temporary, but the interests of the State are permanent, Minister for Industries remarked

title: Work for 90% public satisfaction in delivery of government services, Chief Minister tells officials
content: While appreciating officials of a few departments, the Chief Minister asks a few others to improve stating that performance records will be strictly monitored

title: Hope courts take up coal mining in ‘no-go’ forests of Singrauli with sensitivity: Jairam Ramesh
content: Jairam Ramesh said in May 2025, the Union Ministry of Environment, Forests and Climate Change "predictably cleared" a proposal from the "Modani empire" to mine coal

title: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets PM Modi
content: Secretary of State Rubio, accompanied by U.S. Ambassador to India Sergio Gor, discussed U.S.-India ties on security, trade and critical technologies with Mr. Modi

title: Bandi Sanjay hands over job letters at Rozgar Mela in Anantapur
content: The Rozgar Mela was organised as part of the nation-wide initiative to distribute appointment letters to over 51,000 youths across 47 locations

title: Row erupts over appointment of Kerala Chief Electoral Officer Rathan U. Kelkar as Secretary to CM V.D. Satheesan
content: Formal orders were issued on Saturday appointing Rathan U. Kelkar as Secretary to the Chief Minister .

title: Delhi HC allows Vinesh Phogat to compete in Asian Games trials, pulls up WFI for ‘vindictive’ conduct
content: A Bench of Chief Justice D.K. Upadhyaya and Justice Tejas Karia directed that the trials be video-recorded and ordered the nomination of two independent observers from the Sports Authority of India (SAI) and the Indian Olympic Association (IOA) to oversee the process.

title: Congress, SP to face another rude shock in Uttar Pradesh polls: BJP’s Mukhtar Abbas Naqvi
content: BJP leader Mukhtar Abbas Naqvi targeted the Congress and the SP, saying ‘Rahul (Gandhi) and Company’ has become a ‘playschool of professional political bashers’

title: Karnataka to soon get India’s second largest international cricket stadium
content: The move comes despite opposition from a section of farmers and environmentalists who say the proposed stadium, which will come up close to the Karadikkal-Mahadeshwara wildlife corridor, will have an adverse impact on the environment.

title: Financial decisions will rest with elected leaders: Sonam Wangchuk on Ladakh’s agreement with Centre
content: He noted that earlier 90% of the UT’s budget was decided by the LG who is not an elected representative

title: Sonam Wangchuk calls himself ‘honorary cockroach’, urges government to hear voices of youth
content: Voicing support for the online cockroach movement by the self-styled Cockroach Janata Party, Sonam Wangchuk said the campaign should be viewed as a form of democratic feedback and not as a threat

title: Sulur minor girl abduction and murder: Law and order a serious concern in T.N., says Udhayanidhi Stalin
content: PMK leader Anbumani Ramadoss and DMDK general secretary Premallatha Vijayakant also condemned the abduction and murder of a 10-year-old girl in Sulur, and urged the Tamil Nadu government to take stringent action against those responsible

title: From weed to wealth: Assam women turn water hyacinth into livelihoods near rhino habitat
content: Providing people in fringe areas with an alternative source of livelihood facilitates the conservation of Laokhowa-Burhachapori Wildlife Sanctuary

title: U.P. STF busts SSC exam-rigging racket in Greater Noida; seven held
content: The accused were allegedly manipulating SSC examinations for recruitment of constables in the CAPF, and SSF, and riflemen in the Assam Rifles, at an online examination centre operating under the name "Balaji Digital Zone" in the Knowledge Park area

title: Toddler recovers after month-long critical care battle at Bengaluru hospital following road accident
content: The child was admitted to the Paediatric Intensive Care Unit (PICU) at Manipal Hospital Yelahanka after receiving initial emergency treatment elsewhere.

title: Government plans to inaugurate Bhogapuram Airport on July 5: MP
content: MP advises passengers who have already booked tickets from the existing Visakhapatnam Airport for travel after July 8 to verify from which airport their flights would operate

title: Crackdown on Cockroach Janta Party, access to all accounts lost, says founder Abhijeet Dipke
content: Abhijit Dipke linked the action against his platform to its campaign calling for the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: Centre publishes draft rules for VB-G RAM G, invites feedback
content: Framed under Section 33 and other relevant provisions of the Act, the draft rules have been placed in the public domain to facilitate wider stakeholder consultation before they are finalised

title: Assam CM welcomes Muslims’ appeal against cow sacrifice on Bakrid
content: Eidgah and graveyard committees in at least four districts have urged minorities to respect the religious sentiments of non-Muslims by refraining from cow slaughter during the upcoming festival

title: Encounter breaks out in J&K’s Rajouri during major search operations
content: Troops responded swiftly with calibrated action after the contact, leading to an exchange of fire between the two sides, the spokesman said

title: Two workers injured in compressor blast at northwest Delhi factory
content: Police received information about the incident from Vinayak Hospital in Model Town at 9.50 a.m. after the injured workers were brought there for treatment

title: Man reaches Mathura civic body office dressed as cockroach to draw attention to Yamuna pollution
content: Social activist Deepak Sharma wanted to open the eyes of the ‘incompetent’ officials who have virtually turned a blind eye to the Yamuna pollution and the rampant filth in the city

title: Centre turns on West Bengal funds tap after Suvendu Adhikari’s Delhi visit
content: Following Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari's Delhi visit, the Centre has approved Rs 39,000 crore for West Bengal's water resources ministry schemes. Prime Minister Modi pledged support for central projects and economic development, aiming to boost welfare initiatives like Ayushman Bharat and rural employment. Discussions also covered border security with Home Minister Amit Shah.

title: 'Potential to diversify': Rubio pitches stronger US-India energy ties during meeting with PM Modi
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio affirmed Washington's commitment to preventing geopolitical disruptions from impacting global energy markets, particularly amid tensions linked to the Iran conflict. He highlighted US energy exports as a tool to strengthen partnerships, especially with India, aiming to diversify its supply and reduce reliance on other sources.

title: The big AI shock: Have Indian IT sector stocks lost their lustre?
content: In fact, ⁠IT has been the worst-performing major sector in the Indian market over the past 12-18 months. In May, 2026, the Nifty IT index touched a near 3-year low, losing more than a fourth of its value since the start of the year.

title: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has a message for all those predicting AI to fail
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos dismissed fears of an AI bubble, stating that even if current valuations are unsustainable, the long-term benefits will outweigh the risks. He likened the AI boom to the 1990s biotech bubble, where initial losses led to significant advancements.

title: Twisha Sharma's family lawyer claims Samarth Singh 'hid' inside courtroom before arrest
content: Twisha Sharma's family lawyer alleges her husband, Samarth Singh, was 'hidden' in a locked courtroom before his arrest in Jabalpur. Singh was taken into custody by police after arriving to surrender, with a lookout notice and reward previously issued for his arrest. The case intensifies as the High Court allows a second postmortem and the state recommends a CBI probe.

title: Selling a property? Here’s all you need to know about capital gains tax rules
content: Capital gains tax rules on Property changed from July 23, 2024. Long-term gains now face a 12.5% tax without indexation, a shift from the previous 20% with indexation.  However, those who bought property before this date can choose the old or new system.  Exemptions under Sections 54, 54F, and 54EC remain available.

title: Why Jasprit Bumrah struggled in IPL? Jayawardene reveals shocking reason
content: Mumbai Indians coach Mahela Jayawardene revealed Jasprit Bumrah battled a niggle from the T20 World Cup into IPL 2026, impacting his performance.  Despite a slow start, Bumrah regained pace and sharpness in later games, though the team's campaign ended early.  Jayawardene emphasized Bumrah's strong work ethic and recovery.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to California group, and this is why
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: 'Nobody can outdo us in violence': Bangladesh’s student-led NCP issues warning to ruling BNP
content: Bangladesh's National Citizen Party spokesperson Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain issued a stern warning to the ruling party, asserting their readiness for violence if provoked. Following an attack on a party leader, Bhuyain demanded arrests, threatening a generational battle if the government persists with aggression.

title: Found your dream home? Here’s how to check if it fits your budget
content: Buying a home requires careful financial planning beyond just the property price. EMI calculator tools, like those provided by SBI and HDFC, are crucial for assessing affordability. They consider your income, existing loans, and desired loan terms to estimate monthly payments and borrowing capacity, ensuring a smart financial decision and preventing overborrowing.

title: Full breakdown of USCIS memo on change in Green Card rules for H1-B, L-1, F-1 OPT
content: USCIS has issued a new policy memo requiring most foreigners seeking a green card to apply from their home country, shifting from adjustment of status within the U.S. This stricter standard emphasizes weighing negative factors like immigration violations and inconsistent conduct, demanding stronger evidence of "unusual or outstanding" positive ties to the U.S. for those applying domestically.

title: 'Neither unemployed nor lazy but ... ': Sonam Wangchuk calls himself 'an honorary cockroach'
content: Sonam Wangchuk, calling himself an "honorary cockroach," lauded the satirical "Cockroach Janata Party" movement, urging the government to heed youth concerns instead of suppressing digital dissent. He likened the campaign's creative, non-violent approach to political cartoons, advocating for engagement over censorship to prevent frustration from escalating.

title: Vinesh Phogat cleared for Asian Games trials as Delhi HC calls WFI policy unfair
content: Delhi High Court has permitted star wrestler Vinesh Phogat to compete in Asian Games trials, criticizing the Wrestling Federation of India's rigid policy that penalizes athletes returning from maternity leave. The court emphasized fairness, allowing Phogat's participation and ordering transparent, supervised selection proceedings to uphold the sport's integrity and justice.

title: Centre orders Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate premises by June 5
content: The Delhi Gymkhana Club must vacate its 27.3-acre Lutyens' Delhi land by June 5. The government requires the prime location for defence infrastructure and security. The Land and Development Office issued the order, determining the lease. Possession will be taken on June 5. Non-compliance will result in legal action. The club, established in 1913, is in a strategic zone.

title: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi plays only as Impact Player for RR
content: Rajasthan's 15-year-old batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, an Impact Player, faces scrutiny from former India players for not fielding. Assistant coach Trevor Penny defends the decision, citing Sooryavanshi's fielding skills and the strategy to keep him fresh for his explosive batting, which has impressed many.

title: Future wars will span cyber, cognitive domains: CDS Anil Chauhan
content: Future conflicts will span multiple domains including land, sea, air, cyberspace, and cognitive warfare, Chief of Defence Staff Anil Chauhan stated at the inauguration of a defence manufacturing complex in Shirdi. He emphasized that technological advancements like AI and drones are reshaping battlefields, which will extend beyond geographical limits into digital realms.

title: Ivan Toney reacts to shock England World Cup call-up in viral clip
content: England striker Ivan Toney could not hide his excitement after receiving a surprise call-up to England’s FIFA World Cup squad. The Al-Ahli forward was visiting his favourite barber shop in Clapham, South London, shortly after learning the news. His reaction was captured on video and later shared online by HD CUTZ. Toney appeared relaxed and happy as he sat in the barber’s chair while speaking about representing England at the biggest tournament in football.

title: Ravindra reveals how Kohli and Rahane shaped his Test preparation
content: Rachin Ravindra returns home from IPL early. He feels ready for New Zealand's Test matches in Ireland and England.  Ravindra gained valuable insights from top players like Ajinkya Rahane and Virat Kohli.  He believes these conversations and training sessions have prepared him for challenging conditions.  The experience has boosted his confidence for the upcoming red-ball assignments.

title: Uganda confirms 3 new Ebola cases as WHO warns against underestimating outbreak risk
content: Uganda has confirmed three new Ebola cases, bringing the total to five in the current outbreak. The new infections include a driver and a health worker exposed to the first patient, along with a woman from the Democratic Republic of Congo. Authorities are intensifying contact tracing efforts to contain the spread of the rare Bundibugyo strain.

title: Twisha death case: Husband Samarth sent to 7-day police remand; cops say he’s not cooperating
content: Prime accused Samarth Singh in the death of his wife Twisha Sharma was arrested in Jabalpur and remanded to police custody for seven days in Bhopal. Police stated Singh was not cooperating with the investigation. His mother, Giribala Singh, a co-accused, has been issued multiple notices, and police are seeking cancellation of her anticipatory bail.

title: Coco Gauff reveals secret behind her massive popularity before French Open
content: Coco Gauff has spoken about growing up under public attention before beginning her title defense at the French Open. The American star said that living in the spotlight from a young age has not always been easy. Especially when every mistake becomes visible to millions of people. However, Gauff now believes those moments have helped fans connect with her. She said people can relate to seeing athletes struggle, learn, and improve instead of appearing perfect all the time.

title: How Punjab Kings can qualify for IPL 2026 playoffs
content: The IPL 2026 playoff race intensifies as Punjab Kings face a must-win game against Lucknow Super Giants. Even with a victory, their qualification hinges on the outcomes of Rajasthan Royals vs. Mumbai Indians and Kolkata Knight Riders vs. Delhi Capitals matches. Punjab Kings need specific results to advance without relying on net run rate.

title: 'India-US will continue to work closely for global good': PM Modi after meeting Marco Rubio
content: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi, discussing deepening strategic ties across defense, trade, technology, and the Indo-Pacific. Rubio's visit, which includes meetings with Quad foreign ministers, highlights the strong partnership between the two nations, rooted in shared values and cooperation.

title: Need a loan for plot purchase? Interest rates, eligibility & all you need to know
content: Securing a plot of land for your future home or investment is now feasible with bank loans. While similar to home loans, plot loans have distinct terms, including higher interest rates and shorter repayment periods. Lenders prioritize residential plots within city limits, requiring thorough documentation and legal checks to ensure a smooth purchase.

title: Not RCB or SRH! Historic playoff results make this team favourite for title
content: IPL 2026 playoffs are set to be exciting. Gujarat Titans hold a statistical edge to win the title. Royal Challengers Bengaluru finished first but history favors second-placed teams. Sunrisers Hyderabad face a tough Eliminator route. The final stages promise intense competition for the coveted IPL trophy.

title: Have you been told to take thyroid medicines forever? Read this first
content: Thyroid medication isn't always a lifelong sentence. Dr. Vaibhav Gupta explains that temporary inflammation can resolve, allowing for reduced or stopped treatment. However, autoimmune conditions like Hashimoto's often necessitate permanent hormone replacement.  Hyperthyroidism, conversely, aims for remission.  Sudden discontinuation of medication is dangerous; always consult your doctor for adjustments based on TSH tests.

title: 'Haven't bathed for days': Water crisis roils Delhi's Dakshinpuri; 5,000 residents affected
content: Residents of Dakshinpuri's Block 10 have endured a month without running water, forcing women to seek permission for baths at their workplaces. For approximately 5,000 people, daily life now involves long queues for water, with many hauling buckets up multiple floors amidst a severe heatwave.

title: TV stars Jishin Mohan and Ameya get married
content: Jishin Mohan and Ameya Nair have taken a beautiful leap into matrimony at the serene Guruvayur Temple. This intimate affair comes after their captivating presence on Bigg Boss, which drew them into the spotlight. Friends and colleagues from the Malayalam television industry sent their heartfelt congratulations to the couple.

title: '49% followers from Pak': BJP claims CJP holds only 9% support from India
content: The BJP has alleged that nearly half of the social media support for the viral 'Cockroach Janta Party' originates from Pakistan, with less than 10% from India. The party claims the digital campaign, which targets issues like unemployment and exam leaks, is a foreign influence operation aimed at destabilizing India.

title: Rs 64 crore Keni bridge in Bihar shows exposed iron rods, damaged pillars; fears of collapse rise
content: Concerns over the safety of Bihar's Rs 64-crore Keni Bridge have surfaced due to exposed iron rods and damaged pillars, sparking fears of collapse. The Road Construction Department has launched an inquiry and is inspecting all state bridges to assess their condition and lifespan, with strict action promised if irregularities are found.

title: Telangana woman found hanging; husband accused of staging suicide for Rs 1 cr insurance claim
content: A 29-year-old woman was found dead under suspicious circumstances in Khammam district, with family members alleging her husband murdered her for a ₹1 crore insurance policy. They claim the couple faced marital issues and financial distress. Police are investigating domestic violence allegations and the insurance angle, with no murder charges yet invoked.

title: Ranchi college student molested by Army jawan, arrested
content: An Army jawan has been arrested in Ranchi for allegedly molesting a female student. The incident occurred on Friday as the 23-year-old victim was returning from college. Police have registered an FIR and forwarded the 38-year-old accused, from Haryana and posted in Ranchi, to jail.

title: Aline Brosh McKenna on 'The Devil Wears Prada 3': Box office gave us confidence
content: Twenty years after its debut, 'The Devil Wears Prada' returns, reflecting journalism's digital chaos and media's precarious state. Writer Aline Brosh McKenna discusses the sequel's modern reality check, the challenges faced by institutions, and the enduring appeal of its iconic characters. The future of the franchise hinges on audience reception and box office success.

title: Tamil Nadu horror: 10-year-old girl abducted on way to shop, found dead; locals stage protest
content: Police arrested a 33-year-old man, Karthik, in Coimbatore for the kidnapping and murder of a 10-year-old girl. The girl went missing on Thursday and was found dead on Friday. Karthik allegedly abducted her on a two-wheeler. His aide, Mohan Raj, was also detained. Protests demanding strict action were held, and officials assured a thorough investigation.

title: Cockroach Janta Party website taken down: Founder calls govt 'dictatorial'
content: Abhijeet Dipke, founder of the viral Cockroach Janta Party, announced their website was taken down, accusing the BJP-led Centre of dictatorial behavior. Dipke claimed nearly 10 lakh people had joined the satirical platform, with 6 lakh signing a petition for the Education Minister's resignation over the NEET-UG controversy.

title: Heatwave kills 16 in Telangana, minister announces Rs 4 lakh ex-gratia
content: Telangana Revenue Minister Ponguleti Srinivasa Reddy announced that 16 individuals have died due to the prevailing heatwave conditions across the state this summer. The deaths were reported from seven districts, with Jayashankar Bhupalpally recording the highest number. The government will provide an ex-gratia of Rs four lakh to the families of each deceased.

title: India To Become Major Arms Exporter In 25-30 Years: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Saturday said no power can stop India, once considered an importer of weapons, from becoming the biggest exporter in 25-30 years.

title: "It's Not About India": Marco Rubio To NDTV On New US Greencard Rule
content: "Well, it's not about India. It's about the whole world," US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told NDTV today on the new green card policy

title: RG Kar Case: Bengal Starts Departmental Proceedings Against Suspended Doctor
content: The West Bengal government on Saturday initiated departmental proceedings against a doctor suspended following the rape and murder at RG Kar Medical College and Hospital, and also ordered a separate enquiry into his admission to a postgraduate course

title: 3 Girls Drown In Stepwell While Bathing In Madhya Pradesh
content: Three minor girls, including two sisters, drowned while bathing in a stepwell in Madhya Pradesh's Raisen district on Saturday, police said.

title: Explained: Why Buyers Are Suddenly Ditching 1BHK, 2BHK Flats in Noida
content: The real estate market in Gautam Buddha Nagar is witnessing a dramatic transformation, with buyers increasingly choosing spacious luxury apartments over compact homes.

title: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: "Iran War Will Be Solved One Way Or The Other": Marco Rubio To NDTV
content: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: The top US diplomat is scheduled to hold talks with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and attend a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers.

title: 'No More Slums In Mumbai': Eknath Shinde Unveils Massive Cluster Redevelopment Push
content: Shinde said the government has already formed 19 cluster redevelopment zones across the Mumbai Metropolitan Region.

title: Marco Rubio Meets PM Modi, Highlights India-US Progress Across Sectors
content: Security has been stepped up at the 'jhalmuri' stall, which Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited during his campaign for the West Bengal election last month, after its owner allegedly received death threats, police said on Saturday.

title: Worst Coal Mine Disasters In China Since 1950
content: A gas explosion at Liushenyu coal mine in China's northern Shanxi province late on Friday killed at least 90 people.

title: Tamil Nadu Firmly In Control, Vijay's TVK Set For Rajya Sabha Entry
content: Of the 26 Rajya Sabha seats going to the polls, the NDA holds 18, the Congress 4, YSR Congress 3 and Jharkhand Mukti Morcha one

title: Twisha Sharma's Husband Samarth Singh Sent To Seven-Day Police Custody
content: Samarth Singh had allegedly been evading arrest since May 12, the day Twisha Sharma was found dead at her in-laws' home in Bhopal.

title: Explosive Claim By Salon Owner Over Twisha Sharma's Visit Hours Before Death
content: Sharma visited the salon around 3:12 pm on May 12, later which day she would be found hanging back at her in-laws' house in Bhopal.

title: Twisha Sharma Death Case Updates: Twisha Sharma's Husband Sent To 7-Day Police Custody
content: Twisha Sharma Death Updates: The case came under scrutiny as the Madhya Pradesh Police faced criticism for an inefficient probe into the case, while Giribala Singh was accused of exerting undue influence.

title: BJP MP Alleges Police Nexus Fueling Illegal Liquor Trade In Dry Gujarat
content: Bharuch MP Mansukh Vasava alleged that an organised liquor and gambling network is actively operating in the Narmada district.

title: Centre Orders Delhi Gymkhana Club To Hand Over Premises By June 5
content: The Centre has asked Gymkhana Club in Lutyens' Delhi to hand over the premises by June 5, citing that the 27.3-acre plot was required for "strengthening and securing defence infrastructure".

title: The Story Of Twisha Sharma: A Grand Wedding, Slow Isolation, Then Death
content: According to Twisha's family, the signs of trouble emerged alarmingly early, beginning right from the honeymoon.

title: 'Nobody Can Outdo Us In Violence': Students' Party To Bangladesh PM After Supporters Clash
content: According to locals in Jhenaidah, an egg was thrown at National Citizen Party chief coordinator Nasiruddin Patwary, leading to clashes in Jhenaidah town between two groups

title: Ivanka Trump Assassination Plotted As Revenge Killing For Qasem Soleimani
content: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi had allegedly pledged to kill Ivanka in retaliation for the 2020 US drone strike in Baghdad.

title: Operation Sheruwali: Encounter breaks out in J&K's Rajouri during major search ops
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. title: 30 doctors took bribe, but government sends only 27 names for action
content: govt inquiry found 30 doctors guilty of accepting a luxury trip worth a little over Rs 1.9 crore from abbVie Pharma . but when the department sent the list it contained only 27 names . the department did not respond to TOI queries on how or why the names of three doctors disappeared .
